stem: In right triangle ABC, the right angle is at C, and tan(B) = 5/12. Side BC has length 36. Point D lies on AB and point E lies on BC such that triangle DBE is similar to triangle ABC (with the right angle of DBE at E), and DA = 13. What is the length of DE?
rationale: Since tan(B) = AC/BC = 5/12 and BC = 36 (a scale factor of 3 applied to the 5-12-13 right triangle), we get AC = 15 and AB = 39. Triangles ABC and DBE share angle B and both have a right angle, so they are similar with ratio DB/AB. Since DA = 13, DB = 39 − 13 = 26, so the ratio is 26/39 = 2/3. Therefore DE = (2/3) · AC = (2/3) · 15 = 10.
